title: OpenBody Canonical Exercise Registry Entry
description: One canonical base movement: a stable id, display names, classification + variation facets (SPEC §6.3), registry attributes (e.g. MET), and incumbent crosswalk codes (§6.4). Data is CC0.

names.description: Human-readable display names; first is the preferred label.

attributes.description: Registry attributes of the movement (NOT identity) — e.g. MET intensity (§6.2/§6.4).

met.description: Compendium of Physical Activities MET value.

coded.description: Identity-equivalence crosswalk to incumbent systems (§6.4); advisory.

source.description: Provenance of this entry's data (open set; see SOURCES.md). `curated` = fully hand-authored, no third-party seed. `compendium` = the entry's `attributes.met` comes from the Compendium of Physical Activities (facts, cited per edition). `free-exercise-db` = the entry's existence, coverage, and typically its display name(s) derive from a free-exercise-db worklist row (public-domain upstream, pinned in SOURCES.md); the id and the facet classification were authored to OpenBody conventions (§6.2/§6.3) against this registry's own vocabularies, with a per-entry review — not copied field-for-field from upstream. For any value, `id` and `facets` are OpenBody-authored; `source` records what seeded or informed the entry, not a verbatim import.

progressions.description: Ids of harder variations of this entry (e.g. `pull-up` → `muscle-up`). Relationship between entries, not classification of this one.

regressions.description: Ids of easier variations of this entry (e.g. `pull-up` → `lat-pulldown.cable`). Relationship between entries, not classification of this one.

variations.description: Ids of lateral alternatives at similar difficulty (e.g. `squat.barbell.high-bar` ↔ `squat.barbell.front`). Relationship between entries, not classification of this one.

equipment.description: Equipment class (barbell/dumbbell/machine/…). Open token; recommended canon in vocab/equipment.json (§5.9).
